BUNDLE-GEM(1)               General Commands Manual              BUNDLE-GEM(1)

NNAAMMEE
       bbuunnddllee--ggeemm - Generate a project skeleton for creating a rubygem

SSYYNNOOPPSSIISS
       bbuunnddllee ggeemm _G_E_M___N_A_M_E _O_P_T_I_O_N_S

DDEESSCCRRIIPPTTIIOONN
       Generates a directory named GGEEMM__NNAAMMEE with a RRaakkeeffiillee, GGEEMM__NNAAMMEE..ggeemmssppeecc,
       and other supporting files and directories that can be used to develop
       a rubygem with that name.

       Run rraakkee --TT in the resulting project for a list of Rake tasks that can
       be used to test and publish the gem to rubygems.org.

       The generated project skeleton can be customized with OPTIONS, as
       explained below. Note that these options can also be specified via
       Bundler's global configuration file using the following names:

       •   ggeemm..ccoocc

       •   ggeemm..mmiitt

       •   ggeemm..tteesstt




OOPPTTIIOONNSS
       ----eexxee, ----bbiinn, --bb
              Specify that Bundler should create a binary executable (as
              eexxee//GGEEMM__NNAAMMEE) in the generated rubygem project. This binary will
              also be added to the GGEEMM__NNAAMMEE..ggeemmssppeecc manifest. This behavior is
              disabled by default.

       ----nnoo--eexxee
              Do not create a binary (overrides ----eexxee specified in the global
              config).

       ----ccoocc  Add a CCOODDEE__OOFF__CCOONNDDUUCCTT..mmdd file to the root of the generated
              project. If this option is unspecified, an interactive prompt
              will be displayed and the answer will be saved in Bundler's
              global config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use.

       ----nnoo--ccoocc
              Do not create a CCOODDEE__OOFF__CCOONNDDUUCCTT..mmdd (overrides ----ccoocc specified in
              the global config).

       ----cchhaannggeelloogg
              Add a CCHHAANNGGEELLOOGG..mmdd file to the root of the generated project. If
              this option is unspecified, an interactive prompt will be
              displayed and the answer will be saved in Bundler's global
              config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use. Update the default with bbuunnddllee
              ccoonnffiigg sseett ----gglloobbaall ggeemm..cchhaannggeelloogg <<ttrruuee||ffaallssee>>.

       ----nnoo--cchhaannggeelloogg
              Do not create a CCHHAANNGGEELLOOGG..mmdd (overrides ----cchhaannggeelloogg specified in
              the global config).

       ----eexxtt==cc, ----eexxtt==ggoo, ----eexxtt==rruusstt
              Add boilerplate for C, Go (currently go-gem-wrapper
              _h_t_t_p_s_:_/_/_g_i_t_h_u_b_._c_o_m_/_r_u_b_y_-_g_o_-_g_e_m_/_g_o_-_g_e_m_-_w_r_a_p_p_e_r based) or Rust
              (currently magnus _h_t_t_p_s_:_/_/_d_o_c_s_._r_s_/_m_a_g_n_u_s based) extension code
              to the generated project. This behavior is disabled by default.

       ----nnoo--eexxtt
              Do not add extension code (overrides ----eexxtt specified in the
              global config).

       ----ggiitt  Initialize a git repo inside your library.

       ----ggiitthhuubb--uusseerrnnaammee==GGIITTHHUUBB__UUSSEERRNNAAMMEE
              Fill in GitHub username on README so that you don't have to do
              it manually. Set a default with bbuunnddllee ccoonnffiigg sseett ----gglloobbaall
              ggeemm..ggiitthhuubb__uusseerrnnaammee <<yyoouurr__uusseerrnnaammee>>.

       ----mmiitt  Add an MIT license to a LLIICCEENNSSEE..ttxxtt file in the root of the
              generated project. Your name from the global git config is used
              for the copyright statement. If this option is unspecified, an
              interactive prompt will be displayed and the answer will be
              saved in Bundler's global config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use.

       ----nnoo--mmiitt
              Do not create a LLIICCEENNSSEE..ttxxtt (overrides ----mmiitt specified in the
              global config).

       --tt, ----tteesstt==mmiinniitteesstt, ----tteesstt==rrssppeecc, ----tteesstt==tteesstt--uunniitt
              Specify the test framework that Bundler should use when
              generating the project. Acceptable values are mmiinniitteesstt, rrssppeecc
              and tteesstt--uunniitt. The GGEEMM__NNAAMMEE..ggeemmssppeecc will be configured and a
              skeleton test/spec directory will be created based on this
              option. Given no option is specified:

              When Bundler is configured to generate tests, this defaults to
              Bundler's global config setting ggeemm..tteesstt.

              When Bundler is configured to not generate tests, an interactive
              prompt will be displayed and the answer will be used for the
              current rubygem project.

              When Bundler is unconfigured, an interactive prompt will be
              displayed and the answer will be saved in Bundler's global
              config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use.

       ----nnoo--tteesstt
              Do not use a test framework (overrides ----tteesstt specified in the
              global config).

       ----ccii, ----ccii==cciirrccllee, ----ccii==ggiitthhuubb, ----ccii==ggiittllaabb
              Specify the continuous integration service that Bundler should
              use when generating the project. Acceptable values are ggiitthhuubb,
              ggiittllaabb and cciirrccllee. A configuration file will be generated in the
              project directory. Given no option is specified:

              When Bundler is configured to generate CI files, this defaults
              to Bundler's global config setting ggeemm..ccii.

              When Bundler is configured to not generate CI files, an
              interactive prompt will be displayed and the answer will be used
              for the current rubygem project.

              When Bundler is unconfigured, an interactive prompt will be
              displayed and the answer will be saved in Bundler's global
              config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use.

       ----nnoo--ccii
              Do not use a continuous integration service (overrides ----ccii
              specified in the global config).

       ----lliinntteerr, ----lliinntteerr==rruubbooccoopp, ----lliinntteerr==ssttaannddaarrdd
              Specify the linter and code formatter that Bundler should add to
              the project's development dependencies. Acceptable values are
              rruubbooccoopp and ssttaannddaarrdd. A configuration file will be generated in
              the project directory. Given no option is specified:

              When Bundler is configured to add a linter, this defaults to
              Bundler's global config setting ggeemm..lliinntteerr.

              When Bundler is configured not to add a linter, an interactive
              prompt will be displayed and the answer will be used for the
              current rubygem project.

              When Bundler is unconfigured, an interactive prompt will be
              displayed and the answer will be saved in Bundler's global
              config for future bbuunnddllee ggeemm use.

       ----nnoo--lliinntteerr
              Do not add a linter (overrides ----lliinntteerr specified in the global
              config).

       ----eeddiitt==EEDDIITT, --ee==EEDDIITT
              Open the resulting GEM_NAME.gemspec in EDIT, or the default
              editor if not specified. The default is $$BBUUNNDDLLEERR__EEDDIITTOORR,
              $$VVIISSUUAALL, or $$EEDDIITTOORR.

       ----bbuunnddllee
              Run bbuunnddllee iinnssttaallll after creating the gem.

       ----nnoo--bbuunnddllee
              Do not run bbuunnddllee iinnssttaallll after creating the gem.

SSEEEE AALLSSOO
       •   bundle config(1) _b_u_n_d_l_e_-_c_o_n_f_i_g_._1_._h_t_m_l





                                 February 2026                   BUNDLE-GEM(1)
